lead_profile_text: Switching from lodger arrangement to STL. NHS physiotherapist building private clinic. 2 double bedrooms + small third room (potential storage). Friend currently lodging, moving out in ~6 months. Mortgage remortgage due September 2026 — weighing holiday let / BTL company structure / clear £25k balance. Moving in with partner 2 streets away. Primary goal: consistent passive income with minimal hassle to fund private clinic transition. Not interested in portfolio growth.
emotional_profile: Measured and financially analytical. Clear lifestyle motivation — reduce NHS dependency by building passive income to fund private clinic. Not interested in portfolio growth. Prefers simplicity and maximum flexibility. Concerned about guest quality (wants NHS/healthcare workers, worried about parties).
delay_trigger_phrases: I'll need to speak to my mortgage broker about this
Away mid-June to end of July
Prefer clearing the mortgage for maximum flexibility and simplicity
delay_timeline: September 2026 (mortgage decision); friend departs October/November 2026; target live date November 2026 to capture Chester Christmas markets
green_light_types: Clear lifestyle motivation (reduce NHS hours, fund private clinic)
Chester described as one of most consistent income projections in England
Action plan responses showing 'happy with all of it'
Natural live date aligns with Chester Christmas markets (November/December)
green_light_trigger_phrases: consistent passive income with minimal hassle
I want the flexibility to reduce my hospital days
not interested in scaling or leveraging
prefer clearing the mortgage for maximum flexibility and simplicity

top_objections.text: Getting the right type of guests — specifically wants NHS and healthcare workers, worried about party guests or problem tenants
top_objections.zac_response: Countess of Chester Hospital proximity makes NHS and healthcare workers the natural primary guest. Guest screening with deposits and ID checks filters out problem guests.

top_objections.text: Guest damage and theft — rated as biggest concern in action plan pre-screening
top_objections.zac_response: £200 security deposit, ID checks, damage history screening, £100k damage insurance per stay.

top_objections.text: Mortgage structure complexity — unclear which route to take (holiday let / BTL company / clear £25k balance) before committing
top_objections.zac_response: Stayful can operate under any mortgage structure. Recommended speaking to mortgage broker. September remortgage window is natural decision point.

top_objections.text: Council tax premium during transition period — wants to understand timeline to zero rates
top_objections.zac_response: Council tax premium applies initially but transitions to zero business rates after approximately 1 year of STL operation.
Action plan responses showed 'happy with all of it' — terms not a blocking concern.

top_questions.text: What income can Chester city centre generate via STL?
top_questions.answer: £1,700/month average (£1,200-£2,000 range). £165 nightly rate, 65% occupancy. One of the most consistent income projections in England. Demand drivers: 400+ events annually, University of Chester, Countess of Chester Hospital, Chester Racecourse.

top_questions.text: What type of guests will the property attract?
top_questions.answer: Chester is a major healthcare destination — Countess of Chester Hospital drives strong NHS and healthcare worker demand year-round. Professional guests dominate.

top_questions.text: What happens with council tax when switching to STL?
top_questions.answer: Council tax premium applies initially during transition period, then transitions to zero business rates after approximately 1 year of STL operation.

top_questions.text: How does mortgage structure affect the ability to short-let?
top_questions.answer: Most residential mortgages don't permit short-let — options are holiday let mortgage, BTL with company structure, or clearing the balance. Speak to mortgage broker before committing. September remortgage is the natural decision window.
Full guest communication, cleaning coordination, monthly statements 1st-5th, Slack channel, booking calendar access, quarterly performance calls.
6-month initial term then 3-month rolling notice. No long-term lock-in after initial period.

what_moved_them: Chester demand consistency — described as one of most consistent income projections in England
Countess of Chester Hospital as demand driver for NHS guests — directly matches his preference
Quarterly performance calls and monthly statements — low-hassle management structure
Passive income framing connecting to reducing hospital days and funding private clinic
what_fell_flat: Mortgage structure complexity — left unresolved, requires external broker advice
No urgency lever — no special offer or expiry date

conversion_gaps.gap: Mortgage structure decision blocking commitment — needs broker advice before proceeding
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Connect Sam with mortgage broker contacts specialising in holiday let / STL structures. Follow up in August with mortgage decision confirmed.

conversion_gaps.gap: No urgency lever — no offer or expiry date created
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: At August follow-up, frame Chester Christmas markets (November/December) as the key seasonal opportunity — tie a time-sensitive offer to the autumn sign date.

conversion_gaps.gap: Slow engagement pace — no contact until early August
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Single mid-July check-in message. Emphasise Christmas markets seasonal opportunity. No pressure — respect that Sam is away until end of July.

zac_income_intro: £1,700/month average — one of the most consistent income projections we see in England. Chester has 400+ events annually, a major hospital, a university, and a racecourse — demand is year-round, not seasonal.
zac_transition_phrases: The Countess of Chester Hospital means you get NHS and healthcare workers as your primary guest — exactly the profile you want
The mortgage question is something your broker needs to sign off on — but we can work with whatever structure you land on
Chester Christmas markets in November and December are a major uplift — that's the window you'd want to be live for
zac_reassurance_phrases: This is completely passive once set up — you don't need to do anything
Monthly statements land 1st-5th every month — always clear what's coming in
£200 deposit and ID checks mean you get professional guests, not party groups
rent_mortgage: Mortgage in place (remortgage due September 2026, ~£25k balance)
